metadata.methodology: Canonical complaint list built from the repository's prior CSV export plus a targeted public-source refresh through 2026-04-19. Explicitly cross-posted entries are retained for provenance but excluded from totals.

cases.title: Deposited $330 via Ethereum
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Deposited $330 via Ethereum. Placed minimal $10 wager at 1.01 odds. After withdrawal request and KYC approval, admin 'Razer' declared 'We don't accept fraud here unfortunately, funds are forfeited' with dismissive GIF. Account deleted.
cases.rollbit_response: Razer: 'We don't accept fraud here unfortunately, funds are forfeited' + GIF

cases.title: Deposited $10,000 BTC after sportsbook launch
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Deposited $10,000 BTC after sportsbook launch. Claims support confirmed multi-accounting and VPN usage were permitted. Account immediately limited upon placing bets. Withdrawal denied citing mandatory wagering requirements.
cases.rollbit_response: Admin 'Razer' responded: 'We appreciate you depositing some of the funds back' - interpreted as mocking.

cases.title: Balance of $350 disappeared suddenly during slot play
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Balance of $350 disappeared suddenly during slot play. Support claimed 'no issues with your balance'. OP posted YouTube video evidence but deleted account before resolution. Community skeptical due to visual bug-like behavior in video.

cases.title: Account permanently disabled
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Account permanently disabled. Rollbit claimed 'sportsbook abuse' for value-betting at low odds (1.003-1.02) to qualify for rakeback rewards. Rollbit admin 'Razer' justified seizure saying user 'finished in profit on Rollbit'. User had bet history across CSGO, MMA, basketball, Dota 2, esports.
cases.rollbit_response: Razer stated: 'standard Sportsbook abuse case, flagged by our Sportsbook provider. They were prolifically abusing bonus features. This was not at the expense of the player as they finished in profit.'

cases.title: User fully KYC verified before depositing
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: User fully KYC verified before depositing. After sports betting wins, accused of multi-accounting. Support stopped responding to withdrawal requests for months.
cases.rollbit_response: Razer cited multi-account violations. Offered referral to Curacao licensing authority.

cases.title: Deposited via company hot wallet through FTX partnership in July 2022
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Deposited via company hot wallet through FTX partnership in July 2022. After 15-month lock and KYC including video call with 'SmokeyLisa', Rollbit sent withdrawal to original FTX deposit address without confirming access. FTX had collapsed, making address inaccessible. User provided Exodus wallet alternative during verification but was ignored.

cases.title: Belgian user, 2+ years on platform, wagered ~$125,000
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Belgian user, 2+ years on platform, wagered ~$125,000. Won $27,000. Belgium added to restricted countries after account creation without notification. Was honest with support about situation. Withdrawal eventually processed, then IP banned.

cases.title: Won ~$4,000 on sportsbook
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Won ~$4,000 on sportsbook. Withdrawal of 25.7 SOL requested. Additional $4,500 deducted from balance. After posting complaint, resolved within 1 day after 'hell of verification and details'.

cases.title: Scammed of EUR 17,954
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior Trustpilot extraction. The historical export preserved reviewer/date/amount fields but not stable per-review URLs, so the corpus retains the main review page as the public source locator.
cases.summary: Scammed of EUR 17,954. Filed police report. Going to court. Lawyer reached out. Platform's own lawyer allegedly stated they were illegal. Claims it is illegal to operate under Curacao licence in UK/Europe.

cases.title: $4,000+ in account

cases.title: Won over $50,000 from Spain vs England Euro 2024 sports betting
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Won over $50,000 from Spain vs England Euro 2024 sports betting. 2.5+ year user, ~$80,000 total losses prior. Account locked immediately after win for KYC. Completed KYC levels 1-5 including proof of income. Muted in public chat for discussing KYC status. Lost $3,000 in missed daily rakeback ($400-500/day) during suspension. After 6 days, told 'third-party investigation' in progress.
cases.rollbit_response: Claimed 'formal request from third-party' for the account. Directed to legal@rollbit.com.

cases.title: Played from Spain (restricted country) for 3-4 months
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Played from Spain (restricted country) for 3-4 months. Made ~100 bets. 5 successful withdrawals while unprofitable. When requested EUR 5,000 withdrawal after becoming profitable, KYC triggered. Support member 'Benji' stated: 'you are in significant profit here on Rollbit. The funds have been forfeited.'
cases.rollbit_response: Selective enforcement - allowed deposits and losing play from restricted country but blocked profitable withdrawal.

cases.title: $1,200 deposit seized

cases.summary: $1,200 deposit seized. Banned for 'ban evasion' related to friend's account KYC.

cases.title: Opened 70x leverage position with $44,000 on BTC futures
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Opened 70x leverage position with $44,000 on BTC futures. Platform went offline for 'scheduled maintenance' 30 min before US market open with minimal notice (10 min Telegram pin only). Before maintenance: +$2,000 profit at BTC $98,100. During maintenance: BTC dumped to $97,200. Position loss when site returned: -$36,000. Liquidation price: $96,800. User reports $250,000 cumulative losses over 3 months.
cases.rollbit_response: Rollbit reimbursed traders whose positions were liquidated during maintenance but refused compensation because this user's trade remained 'live' after maintenance ended. Directed to legal@rollbit.com.

cases.summary: Deposited ~$3,000 in crypto. Balance grew to $14,800 through sports betting. Completed 5-step KYC. Unable to withdraw. Rollbit claimed more accounts existed. No evidence provided.
cases.rollbit_response: Casino Guru complaints team contacted Rollbit multiple times. Rollbit failed to respond. Complaint closed as UNRESOLVED.

cases.title: Account suspended without clear explanation
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Account suspended without clear explanation. Both deposited funds and profits confiscated. No response from support.
cases.rollbit_response: No response to Casino Guru mediation attempts.

cases.title: Polish player
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Polish player. $111 withdrawal denied. Accused of multiple accounts. Passed verification. Only one account.

cases.title: Lost ~$25,000 over 2 months
cases.notes_on_verification: Imported from the repository's prior corpus with a direct public source URL.
cases.summary: Lost ~$25,000 over 2 months. Won back a fair portion. Casino suspended account and 'forfeited' over $6,500, claiming 'abusing site features'.

cases.title: Legal lawsuit against owners Daniel Dixon and Jose Llisterri
